intermediate.note: Locked: the Dec 17, 2015 low is independently corroborated by CNN Money's contemporaneous reporting (see module docstring), and its degree naming follows Lara's (elliottwavegold.com) real-time Intermediate-degree count of the decline that ends here -- not subject to promotion. Unlike ^GSPC's GSC (III), no (III)-equivalent peak label is offered for gold's own real 2011 high -- Lara's own cited framing characterizes 2011-2015 as a corrective sequence rather than naming 2011 as a specific counterpart turn.

labels.note: start of (V) = the (IV) trough, same locked convention as ^DJI's Cycle (V) (dashboard_build_spec.md §20), two degrees lower

primary_label.description: Project-derived hypothesis: a single Minor-degree advance in progress since the 2015-12-17 Intermediate (IV) trough, per the Intermediate (V) framing above. NOT subdivided into Minor 1-5 internal waves here -- same reasoning as ^DJI's/^IXIC's own Primary primary_label: the only pivot detector available ('slow' scale, Intermediate/40wk-18mo degree) does not resolve finer-than-Intermediate structure distinctly, so asserting specific Minor I-V turn dates from it would overstate precision. Left for human review on the deep-history panel. Note this advance includes a sharp, real, still-recent swing (a spike to an all-time closing high near $510 on 2026-01-29 followed by a fast pullback into the $370s by 2026-07-02) -- a genuine, large intra-advance correction, not a reversal: the 2015 invalidation level below remains unbroken.

alternates.label: Lara, elliottwavegold.com (16 Nov 2015 gold technical analysis, tracked in real time through the decline): gold's 2011-all-time-high to 2015-low decline is an INTERMEDIATE-degree impulse to the downside, ending with a contracting diagonal in its final (fifth) wave
alternates.caveat: An independent trader's own published, real-time count, not an institutional consensus -- treated as a candidate hypothesis per §3, not ground truth. Written 16 Nov 2015, ~1 month before this bootstrap's own 2015-12-17 fixed low -- i.e. published DURING the final leg down, not reconstructed in hindsight.

alternates.label: CNN Money (Dec 17 2015): gold fell to $1,049.60/oz that day, "the lowest level since October 2009," driven by the Fed's first rate hike since the financial crisis
alternates.caveat: Factual price/date confirmation, not an Elliott Wave source -- included to independently corroborate the fixed anchor's own date and level, not to support the degree-naming claim above (that's Lara's citation).

invalidation.basis: A confirmed daily close below the real 2015-12-17 low would invalidate the Intermediate (V)/Minor-in-progress hypothesis.

history_note: GLD.json covers 2004-11-18 to 2026-07-02 (5438 rows) -- real intraday OHLC from its very first row (no close-only prefix, unlike GSPC/DJI). Own-longest-series self-anchor (dashboard_build_spec.md §2: "GLD, SLV, CL, BTC | own longest series"), independent of any other instrument's degree claim -- gold is its own asset class, no equity/bond "principle of commonality" stand-in applies (unlike DIA/QQQ's original ^GSPC stand-in, dashboard_build_spec.md §18.2). The fixed Intermediate (IV)/(V) anchor intentionally omits a (III)-equivalent peak label for gold's own real 2011 high -- see degree.intermediate.note.
